rejectedObservations.observation: Fail the milestone solely because swift-format lint emits many warnings.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate/already-triaged: the configured lint validator exits successfully and mission guidance says not to reopen work for broad formatting churn alone.

nonBlockingIssues.description: `deleteAlarm(_:)` and `deleteAlarm(byId:)` only delete the `WakeAlarm` row. The feature introduced `WakeAlarmSchedule` and `ValidationStateRecord` as separate persistence models keyed by `alarmId`, but neither delete path removes those records or clears their relationships. After deleting an alarm, `fetchSchedule(for:)` and `fetchValidationState(for:)` can still return orphaned rows for the deleted ID, which violates the stated contract that saved alarms round-trip through dedicated models cleanly and will accumulate stale records over time.
nonBlockingIssues.suggestedFix: Extend both alarm deletion paths to remove any `WakeAlarmSchedule` and `ValidationStateRecord` rows for the alarm ID in the same transaction, and add repository tests covering delete cleanup for both dedicated models.
